message.content:
You are updating AsciiDoc spec files for a CLI rename project. Your assignment is the remaining 16 JJSC files — straightforward command name renames and vocabulary swaps.

## Your Files

1. `Tools/jjk/vov_veiled/JJSCFU-furlough.adoc`
2. `Tools/jjk/vov_veiled/JJSCNO-nominate.adoc`
3. `Tools/jjk/vov_veiled/JJSCSL-slate.adoc`
4. `Tools/jjk/vov_veiled/JJSCRL-rail.adoc`
5. `Tools/jjk/vov_veiled/JJSCDR-draft.adoc`
6. `Tools/jjk/vov_veiled/JJSCCH-chalk.adoc`
7. `Tools/jjk/vov_veiled/JJSCMU-muster.adoc`
8. `Tools/jjk/vov_veiled/JJSCVL-validate.adoc`
9. `Tools/jjk/vov_veiled/JJSCRT-retire.adoc`
10. `Tools/jjk/vov_veiled/JJSCSC-scout.adoc`
11. `Tools/jjk/vov_veiled/JJSCGC-get-coronets.adoc`
12. `Tools/jjk/vov_veiled/JJSCGL-garland.adoc`
13. `Tools/jjk/vov_veiled/JJSCRS-restring.adoc`
14. `Tools/jjk/vov_veiled/JJSCRN-rein.adoc`
15. `Tools/jjk/vov_veiled/JJSCNC-notch.adoc`
16. `Tools/jjk/vov_veiled/JJSCLD-landing.adoc`

## Full Rename Mapping

| Old | New |
|---|---|
| jjx_parade | jjx_show |
| jjx_muster | jjx_list |
| jjx_nominate | jjx_create |
| jjx_slate | jjx_enroll |
| jjx_rail | jjx_reorder |
| jjx_furlough | jjx_alter |
| jjx_notch | jjx_record |
| jjx_wrap | jjx_close |
| jjx_rein | jjx_log |
| jjx_scout | jjx_search |
| jjx_retire | jjx_archive |
| jjx_restring | jjx_transfer |
| jjx_garland | jjx_continue |
| jjx_chalk | jjx_mark |
| jjx_curry | jjx_paddock |
| jjx_draft | jjx_relocate |
| jjx_saddle | jjx_orient |
| jjx_get_spec | jjx_get_brief |
| jjx_tally | split into jjx_revise_docket, jjx_arm, jjx_relabel, jjx_drop (use the appropriate one based on context) |

## Vocabulary Changes (presentation layer only)

- "spec" / "specification" when referring to pace text content → "docket"
- "direction" when referring to bridle execution guidance → "warrant"
- --full flag → --detail

IMPORTANT: Do NOT change "spec" when it refers to the AsciiDoc document itself, the JJSA specification, or the word "specification" in general English. Only change when it means the pace text field content.

## Instructions

1. Read each file
2. Search for any old command names (jjx_parade, jjx_muster, jjx_tally, etc.) in prose text and examples — rename per mapping
3. Search for vocabulary terms (spec/specification meaning pace text, direction meaning bridle guidance, --full) — replace per vocabulary table
4. Preserve AsciiDoc attribute references like `{jjdt_coronet}`, `{jjdo_parade}` etc. — these are linked terms, don't modify them even if they contain old names
5. Preserve `// ⟦...⟧` annotation comments — these are type annotations, don't modify them
6. Some files may have NO changes needed — that's fine, just skip them and report "no changes"
7. For jjx_tally references, choose the appropriate replacement based on context:
   - Updating pace text → jjx_revise_docket
   - Setting bridled state → jjx_arm
   - Renaming pace → jjx_relabel
   - Abandoning pace → jjx_drop
   - If generic reference to tally capabilities → list all four replacements

## Wrap Discipline

DO NOT run /jjc-pace-wrap or mark the pace complete. When work is done:
1. Report what changes you made to each file (or "no changes" for unchanged files)
2. STOP — the calling agent will handle commits and wrap
